nodes.context: English crime gang based in South London in the 1960s, involved in racketeering, extortion, torture, and drug trafficking.
Notorious East London gang led by the Kray twins, involved in organized crime and turf wars.
East End gang that George Cornell originally belonged to before joining the Richardsons.
Manchester-based criminal group, alleged member Dougie Flood owned Mr Smith's Club.

nodes.original_text_name: South London gang led by Billy Hayward
nodes.context: South London gang involved in the brawl at Mr Smith's Club.

edges.context: Turf war and feud in the mid-to-late 1960s, culminating in shootings and killings.
George Cornell left the Watney Streeters and joined the Richardson Gang.
Eddie Richardson and Frankie Fraser were asked to protect Mr Smith's Club, owned by Quality Street Gang member Dougie Flood.
Brawl at Mr Smith's Club between the Richardson Gang and the Hayward Gang led to shootings and arrests.
